_comment: Source of truth for tracker.tasks in data/project-data.js. Edit here, then run: node scripts/sync-tasks.js

tasks.title: Allow the header icon to be customised by the user
tasks.notes: The icon left of the project title is currently a hardcoded SVG. Let users swap it for an emoji, an uploaded image, or a preset icon — similar to how Notion lets you set a page icon. Store the selection in localStorage alongside the project name.

tasks.title: Add redo functionality (Cmd+Shift+Z)
tasks.notes: Follow-on to the undo system. Implement a redo stack that re-applies the last undone action. Undo and redo stacks should stay in sync: redoing after a new action clears the redo stack.

tasks.title: Persist undo history across page refresh using sessionStorage
tasks.notes: The undo stack currently lives in memory and is lost on reload. Serialise it to sessionStorage so users can undo after an accidental page refresh within the same browser tab session.

tasks.title: Undo drag-and-drop reorders in list and board view
tasks.notes: Currently only lane changes via moveTask() are tracked. Drag-to-reorder within the list (manual sort) and board column reordering are not yet undoable. Extend the undo stack to cover these cases.

tasks.title: Show a mini undo history pop-up (last 5 actions)
tasks.notes: Add a small hoverable history indicator near the undo toast, or inside the settings popover, that lists the last 5 undoable actions by description. Clicking an entry undoes back to that point.

tasks.title: Design the OBM Freemium SaaS Architecture
tasks.notes: Brainstorm cloud syncing strategies and gamified business structure for the online-tier SaaS version.

tasks.title: Draft 'Business in a Box' onboarding questionnaire
tasks.notes: Draft the set of questions required from users so the system can dynamically render their business plan and task board.

tasks.title: Remove legacy Mac Control Center task references from seed data
tasks.notes: Tasks MCC-004 through MCC-016 reference a macOS app (MacControlCenter) that is unrelated to LBM. These should be deleted from the seed data or replaced with LBM-relevant backlog items before public release. Quick win — unblocks public GitHub release.

tasks.title: Add screenshots and visual walkthrough to README and docs
tasks.notes: There are no screenshots of the running app. Add at least one screenshot of List View, Board View, and the Detail Panel to the README and/or a new docs/SCREENSHOTS.md. This is the most impactful thing for first-time users evaluating the project.

tasks.title: Cross-browser compatibility testing
tasks.notes: Test the full app (List View, Board View, Detail Panel, Docs tab, Resources tab) in Chrome, Firefox, and Safari. Document any inconsistencies. The drag-and-drop system and contenteditable notes editor are the most likely failure points across browsers.

tasks.title: Verify all internal doc links are correct
tasks.notes: The README and several docs reference each other by relative path. After the docs reorganisation, do a link audit: open each linked file from the Docs tab and from the README to confirm nothing is broken.

tasks.title: Create CONTRIBUTING.md for open-source handoff
tasks.notes: Before making this repo public, write a short CONTRIBUTING.md covering: how to run the app locally, how to add a feature (point to SKILL.md), how to add a keyboard shortcut (point to SKILL_ADD_SHORTCUT.md), how to submit a PR, and code style conventions (vanilla JS, no build step, dark-only CSS).

tasks.title: Expose window.LBM public API in task-app.js
tasks.notes: Add window.LBM = { addTask(), getTasks() } inside the IIFE after init(). addTask() uses normalizeTask() internally and triggers render() + highlightNewRow(). This is the foundation for AI and cross-project task creation.

tasks.title: Create SKILL_ADD_TASK.md
tasks.notes: Claude-facing skill file that defines trigger phrases ("Add this to the task board", etc.) and inference rules for urgency/value/area from natural language. Claude reads this and generates window.LBM.addTask() commands.

tasks.title: Create CLAUDE_INTEGRATION_GUIDE.md
tasks.notes: Guide for using LBM alongside other development projects. Covers the two-tab workflow, cross-project task logging, and guidance for Claude on when to generate commands vs. modify source files.

tasks.title: Add voice input (Web Speech API) to task creation modal
tasks.notes: Mic button next to the Title field in the New Task modal. Speech fills the title; applyVoiceInference() auto-sets urgency and area based on keyword detection. Three visual states: idle, listening (purple pulse), error (red). Gracefully disabled in Firefox.

tasks.title: Create docs/AI_TASK_CREATION.md
tasks.notes: User-facing documentation for the docs viewer covering both AI task creation methods: Claude Code skill and voice input. Includes window.LBM API reference, field table, inference tables, and 3 example console commands.

tasks.title: Create docs/VOICE_COMMANDS.md
tasks.notes: User-facing voice input guide for the docs viewer. Covers how to use the mic button, browser support matrix, urgency/area keyword tables, and tips.

tasks.title: Update data/docs-content.js and data/project-data.js with new docs
tasks.notes: Add pre-rendered cache entries for AI_TASK_CREATION.md and VOICE_COMMANDS.md to docs-content.js. Add new docs and skills entries to project-data.js. Update cached CLAUDE.md and SKILL.md content to reflect new sections.

tasks.title: Update CLAUDE.md with task creation triggers and file map
tasks.notes: Add ADDING TASKS VIA AI section with trigger phrases and API reference. Update file map to include SKILL_ADD_TASK.md, CLAUDE_INTEGRATION_GUIDE.md, and new docs files.

tasks.title: Shortcut Management System — Phase 1: Registry + Settings UI
tasks.notes: Central shortcut registry in localStorage, Settings UI panel to view/edit/reset any shortcut, live key combo recorder, and internal conflict detection. Full spec in docs/ROADMAP_SHORTCUTS.md. Not on the immediate revenue path — deprioritised until public launch milestones are hit.

tasks.title: Add due date field with overdue visual indicator
tasks.notes: Single date field on the task model. Filter bar option for 'overdue'. Visual badge on task card and list row when past due. Keep it lightweight — plain date input is fine, no calendar picker needed. Planned feature from the product roadmap.

tasks.title: Set up public GitHub repo (lbm-free) and push v1
tasks.notes: Create the lbm-free public GitHub repo with MIT license. Clean .gitignore to exclude the private/ folder. Push current main branch. This is Phase 1 of the monetisation launch sequence — the waitlist CTA makes no sense until the repo is public.

tasks.title: Add 'LBM Cloud — coming soon' waitlist CTA in app
tasks.notes: Subtle CTA in the app footer or ⓘ info panel. Links to a Typeform or Google Form waitlist. Goal: 50+ signups to validate demand before building cloud features. Do not build Tier 2 without this signal first. Planned in the monetisation roadmap.

tasks.title: Design and implement Supabase auth + task sync (Pro tier)
tasks.notes: Replace localStorage read/write with Supabase API calls. Add user_id and workspace_id to the task model. Use Supabase Auth for login/signup. The data shape is identical — only the persistence layer changes. Stage 1 of the Tier 2 backend plan. IMPORTANT: Requires LBM-R006 product decisions to be locked first — do not start until persona, pricing, and branding are agreed.

tasks.title: Resolve open product questions before Tier 2 build starts
tasks.notes: Four decisions must be locked before Pro development begins: (1) Paying user persona — solopreneur, small team, or agency? (2) Licence — MIT or AGPL for lbm-free? (3) Pricing model — subscription ($7–9/mo) or one-time ($49)? (4) Branding — keep 'LBM' or rename Pro to 'OBM'? This unblocks LBM-R005.

tasks.title: Implement Stripe billing for Solo Pro tier ($7–9/month)
tasks.notes: Stripe Checkout + Billing Portal integration in lbm-pro. Solo Pro only at launch — no team tier yet. Keep it simple: one product, one price, one plan. Gate Pro features behind auth middleware. Only start after Supabase auth is working (LBM-R005).

tasks.title: Build task search with keyboard-first filtering
tasks.notes: The current search filters visually but has no keyboard shortcut to focus it. Add / to jump to search, Escape to clear, and highlight matched text within task titles. This is a high-frequency interaction for power users and pays off fast.

tasks.title: Add task activity log (who changed what and when)
tasks.notes: Every task edit currently overwrites lastModified with no history. Add a lightweight change log per task — stored in task.history as an array of {date, field, from, to} entries. Visible in the detail panel. Foundation for the Pro activity feed.

tasks.title: Add bulk lane-move action for multi-selected tasks
tasks.notes: Multi-select already works in list view. Extend it with a 'Move to lane' bulk action in the selection toolbar. A small dropdown lets you pick any active lane and moves all selected tasks at once. One undo entry covers the whole batch.

tasks.title: Remove legacy MCC seed tasks before public GitHub release
tasks.notes: Tasks MCC-001 through MCC-016 reference a macOS app (MacControlCenter) unrelated to LBM. Strip these from the seed data and replace with LBM-relevant examples. Quick win — unblocks the public release and cleans up the codebase fast. Must be done before the repo goes public.

tasks.title: Add mobile-responsive layout for list and board views
tasks.notes: The app is currently desktop-only. A responsive pass — collapsing the detail panel on small screens, stacking board columns vertically on mobile, and making the toolbar overflow gracefully — would open LBM to tablet and phone use without a native app.

tasks.title: AI instruction chat section in list view
tasks.notes: Add a dedicated AI chat/instruction panel to the list view. Users can type natural-language commands to Claude to create, update, or move tasks without leaving the app. Future feature — design TBD. High value for power users and a key differentiator for the Pro tier.

tasks.title: Jack
tasks.notes: Test task — maximum urgency and dollar value
tasks.description:
## What to do
This is a test task named Jack. No action required — created to verify task creation flow with urgency 5 and value $25,000.

## Why it matters
Validates that the file-based task creation pipeline works correctly end to end, including sync script and browser reload.

## Why it's valuable
Confirms the $25,000 revenue tier is selectable and displays correctly in the UI. Serves as a reference example for the maximum urgency and value combination.

## Why it's urgent
Test task — added at maximum urgency to verify urgency-5 rendering and priority logic. No real time pressure.

tasks.title: Rotate Neon neondb_owner password for lbm-online
tasks.notes: Connection string was pasted in an AI transcript. Reset the neondb_owner password in Neon console and update .env.local + Vercel env vars once scaffolded.
tasks.description:
## What to do
1. Open the Neon console → project `lbm-online` → Roles → `neondb_owner` → Reset password.
2. Copy the new connection string from the Neon dashboard.
3. Update `DATABASE_URL` in `1_LBM_Online/.env.local` once the Next.js scaffold exists (CTX-02+).
4. If the project has already been deployed to Vercel (CTX-08+), update the `DATABASE_URL` env var in Vercel project Settings → Environment Variables → Production/Preview/Development, then trigger a redeploy.

## Why it matters
The previous connection string (including password) was pasted in an AI chat transcript during the 2026-04-21 handover session. Transcripts may be logged, indexed, or persisted longer than intended. Any leaked DB credential is a direct path to read/write every task, workspace, and user record in the cloud DB.

## Why it's valuable
Protects the entire Phase 1 online build. A compromised neondb_owner can drop tables, exfiltrate user data, and destroy the product's credibility before a single paying customer signs up. This is the cheapest security win on the board — 90 seconds of console work avoids a compounding risk across every later phase.

## Why it's urgent
The password is currently valid and in at least one chat log. Every hour it stays active is an hour a leaked transcript can be weaponised. Do this before the next work session.

tasks.title: Verify 1_LBM_Online repo landed correctly on GitHub
tasks.notes: Open https://github.com/bappygolder/lbm-online and confirm the 14-file initial commit is visible on the main branch.
tasks.description:
## What to do
1. Open https://github.com/bappygolder/lbm-online in a browser.
2. Confirm the latest (initial) commit is `chore(bootstrap): initial schema + Phase 1 build prompts` on branch `main`.
3. Confirm the file tree contains: `.gitignore`, `README.md`, `db/schema.sql`, and `prompts/build/` with `BUILD_INDEX.md` + `CTX-01` through `CTX-10` (12 files in that folder). Total should be 14 files.
4. Spot-check that `README.md` renders cleanly on the GitHub landing page and that no `.env*` files are present in the tree.
5. If anything is missing or a secret file is visible, report back immediately — the push may need to be redone or a file needs to be removed with `git rm --cached`.

## Why it matters
Phase 1 continuation depends on the repo being intact on GitHub. Every CTX-NN prompt starts with `git pull origin main`, so if the initial commit didn't reach origin, the first future session will silently start from a broken base.

## Why it's valuable
Catches a bad push while the context is still fresh (same day as the commit). A missing file discovered weeks later costs context re-derivation; discovered now it's a 30-second fix.

## Why it's urgent
Low urgency in isolation, but should be done before Bappy logs off today — any issue is trivially fixable while the push is still in working memory.

tasks.title: Add 'effort' field (1–10) to task model and UI
tasks.notes: Add an effort property to the task data model (1–10 numeric, similar to urgency). Expose it in: normalizeTask() default, New Task modal select, inline list row editor, board card, detail panel, sort options, and markdown export. Effort represents implementation complexity; complements urgency (time pressure) and value (business impact).
